Take a frying pan to the fire with the olive oil, garlic teeth and the laurel leaf, let it warm up, add the steaks and let them fry on both sides, leaving them preferably barely ironed.
Cut the cakes from the shard in half and bar them inside with mustard. Place the arugula on the lower half, have the steaks and brie cheese sliced, cover with the other halves and serve.

naughty-tuna-rice-teleculinaria.name: Naughty Tuna Rice
instructions: Light a pot with the olive oil and saute the garlic teeth and the chopped onions. Add the pepper paste and tomato pulp and let cook for 2 minutes, stirring at all times.

Add the rice and let cook a little, stirring always. Add the tuna and fish broth, season with pepper and, if necessary, rectify the salt. Let it cook, in mild heat, stirring from time to time, until the rice is on point (about 10 to 12 minutes). If necessary, add some water during cooking.
Remove from the fire, sprinkle with chopped cilantro and serve immediately with ginned green olives.

new-spain-sherry-and-mezcal-cocktail.name: New Spain (sherry And Mezcal Cocktail)
instructions: Add ginger to a mixing glass with the agave and lime juice and muddle. Add sherry and mezcal, and fill with ice. Shake, and finely strain over a large cube of ice into a rocks glass. Grate nutmeg over the top and garnish with the lime wheel.

no-bake-cookies-and-cream-cheesecake.name: No-bake Cookies And Cream Cheesecake
instructions: Separate the cream from the cookies into 2 different bowls.
Place the cookies into a plastic bag and crush them into fine crumbs with a rolling pin. Set aside ½ cup (50g) of the crumbs for later.
Pour the cookie crumbs into a bowl and combine with the melted butter, mixing until the texture is like slightly wet sand.
Press the cookie crumbs in an even layer into the bottom of a 9-inch (23 cm) springform pan. Set aside.
In a medium pot over low heat, mix together the cream cheese, sugar, the cream from the cookies, and vanilla extract, stirring until there are no lumps.
In a measuring cup, mix the milk with the gelatin until dissolved.
Pour the milk into the cream cheese mixture, stirring constantly until the mixture starts to bubble slightly. Remove from heat.
Pour the mixture over the cookie crust in the springform pan. Using a spatula or a spoon, smooth the top.
Sift the saved cookie crumbs on top of the cheesecake in an even layer, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
Unclasp the ring of the springform pan, slice, then serve!
Enjoy!

no-nonsense-matzo-balls.name: No-nonsense Matzo Balls

ingredients.item: Melted schmaltz (chicken fat) or vegetable oil

instructions: Whisk eggs in a medium bowl until no streaks remain. Add schmaltz, broth, dill, pepper, and 1½ tsp. salt and whisk vigorously to combine. Whisk in matzo meal until well combined. Chill at least 35 minutes or up to 2 hours (this is essential so that the matzo meal can hydrate).
Bring 3 qt. water to a boil in a medium pot. Season with remaining 3 Tbsp. salt.

Using dampened hands, roll matzo mixture into 16 balls about 1½" in diameter. It’s okay to really work them into a ball; they won’t get dense—trust us, we tried! Transfer to a small rimmed baking sheet or large plate.

Carefully lower matzo balls into boiling water with a slotted spoon, adjusting heat as needed to maintain a low simmer. Cover pot and simmer over low heat, checking occasionally to make sure water isn’t boiling too rapidly, until balls are very puffed and light in color, 30–40 minutes. Don’t remove them sooner than this; they will be dense in the middle if undercooked. Turn off heat and let balls sit in cooking liquid until ready to serve.
Do Ahead: Matzo balls can be made 2 days ahead. Transfer to an airtight container along with 2–3 Tbsp. cooking liquid and chill.

instructions: It is the nicest, most economical, quick and easy soup you have made.
Clean the onion and carrot, and paste them in the blender along with the tomato.
Heat the olive oil in a small pot, and sauté the garlic for 1 minute.
Remove it from the pot, and sauté the brown fide for 2 minutes to brown, and remove its aromas.
Add the mashed vegetables, potato, hot broth or water, salt and pepper, and boil for 10 minutes.
Serve in a bowl, rub some cheese, or sprinkle with kefalotiri.

nutella-bread-pudding.name: Nutella Bread Pudding
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Butter a pie dish and set aside.
Tear bread into 1- to 2-inch pieces and place in the bottom of the prepared pie dish.
Combine evaporated milk, white sugar, brown sugar, eggs, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and salt in a bowl. Mix well and set aside.
Add melted butter to bread pieces in the pie dish and toss to combine.

Melt chocolate-hazelnut spread in a microwave-safe bowl in a microwave to soften slightly, 15 to 30 seconds. Place dollops on top of bread mixture, spreading to make an even layer. Sprinkle with walnuts. Pour milk-egg mixture on top, making sure bread is completely covered.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 45 to 50 minutes.

nuts-of-kythera-argiro.name: Nuts Of Kythera
instructions: In the mixer's bowl, add the flour, olive oil and salt.
With the dough hook, at low speed, knead for a few minutes to wet the flour with the oil.
When the flour no longer has knots, crush with your hands the fresh yeast into the flour and add the water lukewarm.
Bake at low speed for 7-8 minutes until all the walls of the bowl are removed and a soft, soft and elastic dough is formed.
We take it out, split it into two parts.
We make two long long strawberries and put them in a shallow pan.
Cut into thin slices of 1 cm down.
Cover the baking pan and leave it in a hot spot, until they Rise for about 45′′.
Then baking in a strong ocean at 200°3′ in the air for 35′′.
Let them cool so we can catch them.
We separate by hand by pulling a slice.
Spread them into a baking pan and bake in a low oven at 140°C in the air, about 1 hour, until complete dry and crispy.
After removing the nuts from the oven, let them cool completely.
We keep them in an airtight vessel to keep them crisp and fluffy together.
